stories.title: Iran says it has closed Strait of Hormuz over Israel attacks on Lebanon
stories.body: The US military has disputed Tehran's claim, which comes ahead of US-Iran talks in Switzerland on Sunday.

stories.title: Lebanese turtle conservationist Mona Khalil killed by Israeli strike
stories.body: Mona Khalil, who had refused to leave the beach she had spent years protecting, died from her injuries after the Israeli strike.

stories.title: Meloni tells Trump to 'focus on your own popularity' as row escalates
stories.body: The US president earlier questioned Meloni's popularity after suggesting she "begged" for a photo at G7 summit

stories.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
stories.body: The move comes after weeks of anti-government protests that have caused a shortage of basic goods in Bolivia.

stories.title: Zelensky returns highest Polish honour after award stripped
stories.body: Ukraine's president said his country was open to "engagement" about "difficult and painful chapters of our shared past".

stories.title: Tourist dies in Dominican Republic luxury resort fire
stories.body: Nearly 1,700 people were evacuated from the huge blaze at Viva Wyndham Dominicus Beach in Bayahibe, a popular resort town on the Caribbean coast.

stories.title: Zelensky stripped of highest Polish honour over WW2 name of army unit
stories.body: Ukraine has denounced the move, calling it a "strategic mistake" and "disrespectful".

stories.title: US to stop funding HIV programmes in South Africa
stories.body: More than eight million South Africans are living with HIV – the highest number of any country in the world.

stories.title: Australia confirms first case of H5N1 bird flu as virus reaches every continent
stories.body: Australia was previously the only continent where the H5N1 bird flu strain had not yet been found.

stories.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
stories.body: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

stories.title: James Burrows, legendary director of Cheers and Friends, dies aged 85
stories.body: Best-known as co-creator of sitcom Cheers, Burrows directed more than 1,000 episodes of other classics including the Big Bang Theory and Will and Grace.

stories.title: Boy, 12, wins hearts after trying to check sick chicken into Ethiopian hospital
stories.body: Markos's beloved hen was not responding to any treatments - so he did the only thing he could think of.

stories.title: Morocco captain Achraf Hakimi to stand trial for rape
stories.body: French prosecutors confirm Morocco captain Achraf Hakimi will stand trial for rape, in accusations dating back to 2023.

stories.title: Zimbabwe MPs pass bill to extend president's time in power
stories.body: The proposal would extend Emmerson Mnangagwa's term by two years and scrap direct presidential elections.

stories.title: Six-year-old Ebola patient taken from DR Congo hospital found and 'doing well'
stories.body: Health facilities have come under attack during the current outbreak as a result of misinformation and fear.

stories.title: Mangione's lawyers reverse course on psychiatric defence in state murder trial
stories.body: The defence team reverse course a day after saying they would argue he suffered "extreme emotional disturbance".

stories.title: Do it at home too, women tell Japanese fans who cleaned World Cup stadium
stories.body: Some see a double standard: Japanese men who clean in public while their wives do all the housework.

stories.title: Pétanque player, 68, dies after being 'hit in head with metal boule'
stories.body: The man was allegedly struck by his 81-year-old opponent, who has since been arrested, the public prosecutor says.

stories.title: UK actress charged with importing meth worth almost A$300m into Australia
stories.body: Emaa Hussen, 34, faces life in prison for allegedly trying to smuggle 320kg of meth hidden in bags of charcoal.

stories.title: Asia's richest man Ambani announces what could be India's biggest share sale
stories.body: India's largest telecom operator is expected to raise around $4bn (£3.02bn), according to media reports.

narrative:
🟡 **Mood: Cautious**  
Today's signals reflect a cautious portfolio mood, with structural patterns pointing to incremental positive developments amidst contested and background noise-heavy narratives. While some signals suggest forward momentum, others highlight unresolved tensions and emerging risks.

**1. Structural significance of top signals:**  
The call by African and Caribbean nations for a formal apology for transatlantic slavery underscores a governance-oriented and care-driven push for historical accountability. This signal aligns with broader global trends toward reparative justice, though its categorization as "background noise" suggests limited immediate impact on geopolitical or economic stability. Similarly, Ambani's announcement of what could be India's largest share sale signals achievement-oriented momentum in Asia's financial landscape. However, the speculative framing ("could") tempers its structural weight, as the outcome remains uncertain.

**2. Contested signals and directional ambiguities:**  
Several signals exhibit contested dynamics, particularly those tied to geopolitical and humanitarian tensions. Zelensky's return of Poland's highest honor, following its revocation, reflects a contested narrative around historical memory and sovereignty. While the signal direction is positive, the underlying contest score (-0.88) reveals significant disagreement or unresolved tensions. Similarly, the Dominican Republic resort fire and Australia's first H5N1 bird flu case highlight survival-focused risks, but their low priority scores suggest limited structural impact beyond immediate localized concerns.

**3. Longer-term trajectories:**  
The closure of the Strait of Hormuz, reported as a response to Israeli actions in Lebanon, introduces a critical chokepoint risk for global energy markets. While not a top signal today, this development could escalate into a significant structural disruption if sustained. Additionally, the Bolivian state of emergency reflects ongoing governance fragility in Latin America, a region facing compounded pressures from environmental, economic, and political instability. These signals align with broader trajectories of contested sovereignty and resource-driven tensions.

**4. What to watch next:**  
Key areas to monitor include the operationalization of Ambani's share sale, which could set a precedent for India's financial market dynamics. The African and Caribbean reparative justice push warrants attention for potential ripple effects in international governance frameworks. Finally, the Strait of Hormuz closure and escalating Middle Eastern tensions demand close scrutiny, as they could rapidly shift from background noise to structural disruption with global energy and security implications.

In summary, while today's signals suggest cautious optimism in some areas, contested narratives and emerging risks in others underscore the need for vigilance. The interplay between historical accountability, resource-driven tensions, and governance fragility will likely shape the medium-term outlook.
